Fix RunStatsScreen: fetch own data instead of using stale passed-in runs

Previously RunStatsScreen received runs as a constructor parameter snapshot.
After server restart/sync, the already-pushed screen kept old null cadence data.
Now it fetches its own data on init so cadence/stride charts always show fresh.

commit.message:
Runs: add cadence and stride length over time charts

- Parse gpxtpx:cad (steps/min) and gpxtpx:speed from GPX track points
- Compute avg_cadence (spm) and avg_stride_m = speed*120/cadence per run
- Migration 00010: add avg_cadence, avg_stride_m columns to runs table
- Extracted parse_trkpt, compute_timing, compute_elevation_gain helpers
- Update all INSERT/SELECT queries and import_gpx_run upsert
- RunSummary/RunDetail: expose avgCadence and avgStrideM
- Run stats screen: Cadence trend (spm) + Stride length trend (m) charts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix VS Code install on Trixie: use apt-get to resolve deps

dpkg -i does not handle missing dependencies. Replaced with
apt-get install on the local .deb so transitive deps are pulled
automatically — required after the Bookworm→Trixie migration.

commit.message:
feat!: inline plugin skills config, workspace schema v2 (#233) (#236)

BREAKING CHANGE: skill selection is now configured inline on each
plugin entry instead of top-level disabledSkills/enabledSkills arrays.

- Add PluginSkillsConfig type: string[] (allowlist) or {exclude:string[]} (blocklist)
- Add skills field to PluginEntrySchema object variant
- Add version field to WorkspaceConfigSchema
- Update getAllSkillsFromPlugins and collectPluginSkills to read inline skills
- Add pluginSkillsMode to SkillInfo for caller convenience
- Update workspace-modify and user-workspace write functions to write inline
- Simplify plugin-skills CLI: remove inAllowlistMode detection, use SkillInfo.pluginSkillsMode
- Add migrateWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 / migrateUserWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 called on sync
- Bump to v1.0.0, add CHANGELOG.md

Migration: automatic on next workspace sync. No manual action required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: allagents
repository.description: CLI tool for managing multi-repo AI agent workspaces with plugin synchronization across multiple AI clients.

commit.message:
feat: auto-wrap flat SKILL.md repos as skills (#232) (#234)

When a plugin has no skills/ subdirectory, scan the plugin root for
directories containing SKILL.md and treat each as a skill. This gives
compatibility with the npx skills ecosystem (e.g. vercel-labs/agent-skills)
without any schema changes.

The enabledSkills/disabledSkills schema continues to work unchanged
since the plugin is still tracked as a workspace plugin entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: allagents
repository.description: CLI tool for managing multi-repo AI agent workspaces with plugin synchronization across multiple AI clients.

commit.message:
feat: support installing skills individually (#228) (#231)

- Fix plugin install --skill when plugin already installed: detect
  'Plugin already exists' error and if --skill flags provided, skip
  the install step and proceed directly to enabling skills + sync

- Add top-level `allagents skills` command as shorthand alias for
  `allagents plugin skills` (list/add/remove), giving UX parity
  with `npx skills`

- Enhance `skills add` with --from/-f option: if skill not found in
  installed plugins, installs the specified plugin source first then
  enables the skill (mirrors `npx skills add <pkg> --skill <name>`)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add property-based tests with fast-check for scheduler invariants

Introduces randomized stress-testing of the scheduling engine using fast-check.
Each test generates random inputs (teachers, subjects, quotas, seeds) and
verifies key invariants hold across 50+ randomized runs.

Invariants tested:
- Structural integrity (cells are null or string)
- Schedule dimensions match configuration
- No teacher teaches two classes at the same time slot
- Lab sessions always occupy adjacent pairs (no orphans)
- Lab room not double-booked across classes
- Main subject quotas not exceeded
- Determinism (same seed → same schedule)
- schedulerIsFullyValid() reports no critical violations

Discovery: lab-across-lunch can occur under tight constraints (documented
as known scheduler limitation, tracked statistically).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Stage 1: proposal-to-gdd.js — template-fill GDD generation (10 sections)

Stage 2: gdd-to-issues.js — extended with pipeline:issues label + manifest
Stage 3: implement-game.yml — label-triggered implementation orchestrator
Stage 4+5: build-deploy.yml — game repo template (static/npm auto-detect)
Infra: pipeline-orchestrator.js — state machine for pipeline tracking
Infra: create-pipeline-labels.js — 12 pipeline labels created
Example: chrono-tiles.proposal.md — sample proposal

All scripts tested. Zero Azure cost.

Implement Notezilla features: auth, note CRUD, authorization, tests, Postgres, deployment config

- Added delete notes with confirmation UI
- Implemented show note with dedicated view page
- Configured email/password authentication via Laravel Breeze
- Created clean landing page with call-to-action
- Updated branding to Notezilla throughout
- Migrated database from SQLite to PostgreSQL
- Set up separate test database with Podman
- Configured Laravel Cloud deployment
- Rewrote README with setup and deployment instructions
- Wrote comprehensive feature tests (12 tests for notes CRUD/auth)
- Wrote unit tests for models (5 tests)
- Created authorization policies for note access control
- All 42 tests passing with 105 assertions
- Fixed migrations for Postgres compatibility

Features:
- Note creation, viewing, editing, and deletion
- User authentication with registration and login
- Authorization policies prevent users from accessing other users' notes
- Guest users redirected to login for protected routes
- Production-ready environment configuration

commit.message:
docs(P1-11): Proposal→Prototype workflow architecture

Six-stage autonomous pipeline: text proposal → playable prototype.
Defines input/output contracts, quality gates, failure handling, and
triggering mechanisms for all stages. Label-based state machine for
Ralph monitoring. 12 implementation sub-tasks decomposed for Trinity.

Key decisions:
- @copilot for GDD generation (zero Azure cost)
- Per-game repos under FirstFrameStudios/
- Static-first build (HTML5 Canvas + vanilla JS default)
- Pipeline logic centralized in Syntax-Sorcery

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(infra): add Azure cost alerting system (P1-09)

Three-tier cost alerting via GitHub Actions + Azure CLI:
- 80% (€400): P1 issue → Tank review
- 90% (€450): P0 escalation → Morpheus + founder
- 96% (€480): auto-kill idle VMs/AppServices/containers (respects protected tag)
- Proactive projected overspend alerts via linear extrapolation
- Reusable script with --json and --budget flags
- Dry-run mode for safe testing
- Zero additional Azure cost (GHA free tier)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deflake //rs/tests/networking:canister_http_correctness_test (#9288)

```
$ bazel run //ci/githubstats:query -- last --flaky //rs/tests/networking:canister_http_correctness_test --columns=last_started_at,errors
...
╒════╤═════════════════════════╤══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╕
│    │   last started at (UTC) │ errors per attempt                                                                                                                                                   │
╞════╪═════════════════════════╪══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╡
...
├────┼─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  1 │ Mon 2026-03-09 10:18:50 │ 1: test_http_endpoint_with_delayed_response_is_rejected: Unexpected error: TransportError(Reqwest(reqwest::Error { kind: Request, url: "h...                         │
├────┼─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  2 │ Fri 2026-03-06 10:20:12 │ 1: test_http_endpoint_with_delayed_response_is_rejected: Unexpected error: TransportError(Reqwest(reqwest::Error { kind: Request, url: "h...                         │
├────┼─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  3 │ Fri 2026-03-06 10:14:13 │ 1: test_http_endpoint_with_delayed_response_is_rejected: assertion failed: `Err(RejectResponse { reject_code: SysTransient, reject_messag...                         │
│    │                         │    test_http_endpoint_response_is_within_limits_with_default_max_response_bytes: Request is successful.: RejectResponse { reject_code: SysTransient, reject_messa... │
├────┼─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  4 │ Thu 2026-03-05 21:22:13 │ 1: test_max_possible_request_size_exceeded: Unexpected error: TransportError(Reqwest(reqwest::Error { kind: Request, url: "h...                                      │
╘════╧═════════════════════════╧══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╛
```

## Root Cause

The `test_http_endpoint_with_delayed_response_is_rejected` test flakes
because the IC agent's HTTP transport has a 20-second request timeout
(`AGENT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT`). When the node is under load processing many
concurrent HTTP outcall tests, individual `read_state` polling requests
can exceed this timeout. The `submit_outcall` function panics on any
`AgentError` that isn't a `CertifiedReject` or `UncertifiedReject`,
including `TransportError(TimedOut)`.

## Fix

Add retry logic in `submit_outcall` for transport errors. When a
`read_state` poll times out, we retry the entire `call_and_wait()`.

---

This PR was created following the steps in
`.claude/skills/fix-flaky-tests/SKILL.md`.

---------

Co-authored-by: Kamil Popielarz <kamil.popielarz@dfinity.org>

Adding real weight + real io test to Deepseek MoE module test (#39686)

### Ticket
#39604

### Problem description
test_moe appears to behave materially worse with real weights than with
random weights, and the current explanation is unclear.

### What's changed
Adding real weights to test_moe, after investigation concluded that real
inputs / hidden states are also needed to ensure PCC > 0.99. Deepseek
MoE is dependent on the normalization of inputs, and fails if
normalization is not as expected.

### Checklist

- [x] [![DeepSeek
Tests](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/galaxy-deepseek-tests.yaml/badge.svg?branch=ds-moe-real)](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/galaxy-deepseek-tests.yaml?query=branch:ds-moe-real)

Fix Phase 2 DoD: hitbox groups + hitscan self-hit exclusion

Bug 1 (CRITICAL): HitboxManager._ready() now registers HeadHitbox and
BodyHitbox Area2D nodes into 'head_hitbox' and 'body_hitbox' groups.
Without this, all weapon raycasts' is_in_group() checks returned false,
so no damage ever registered on players (only TargetDummies worked).

Bug 2 (MEDIUM): Added WeaponBase._get_owner_exclusions() which traverses
weapon → WeaponHolder → StickmanController → HitboxManager and returns
the Area2D children's RIDs. All three hitscan weapons (pistol, sniper,
shotgun) now use this helper instead of the broken get_parent() check
that was always failing the CollisionObject2D type guard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sort exercises by most recently used, expose muscle_group in API

- list_exercises now JOINs workout_sets/workouts and orders by

  exercises surface first

- Flutter exercise picker already has muscle-group filter chips and
  preserves API ordering, so no Flutter changes needed

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🚑 修复其他扩展注入 chrome.runtime 导致环境误判的问题 #1280 (#1281)

* 🐛 修复其他扩展注入 chrome.runtime 导致环境误判的问题 #1280

移除 isContent 运行时检测，改为通过 CustomEventMessage.envTag 在构建入口确定环境，
避免其他扩展（如大学搜题酱）向页面注入 chrome.runtime 对象导致 inject 环境被误判为 content 环境。

* Update src/app/service/content/gm_api/gm_api.ts

commit.message:
feat: add context hygiene CI and archive structure

- GitHub Action: squad-size-check.yml (34 lines, pure bash)
  - Fails PR if any .squad/ file > 15KB
  - Warns if any file > 12KB
  - Fails if total .squad/ > 100KB
  - Excludes archive/ and templates/ (not agent context)
- Archive directory with README (agents don't read archives)

Closes #2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add complete Employee Management System (Node.js/Express/SQLite backend + React frontend)

- Backend: Express API with SQLite via better-sqlite3, full CRUD for employees
- Frontend: React app with EmployeeList and EmployeeForm components
- Features: department filtering, create/edit/delete employees
- Backend runs on port 5000, frontend proxies API calls
- Rate limiting on all API routes (express-rate-limit)
- Updated page title and manifest to reflect app name

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: An enterprise employee management system with CRUD operations

commit.message:
fix: address review findings — 6 fixes across code and docs - requirements.txt: uncomment openpyxl as required dependency - detect_agent_sharing_violations.py: use json.dumps() for Teams card violation list escaping instead of manual replace chain - asard_zone_rules.py: collect all zone compliance violations instead of early-returning on first; adds violations list to return dict while preserving backward-compatible violation_type/details keys - zones-and-tiers.md: document Zone 3 individual sharing prohibition and add Sharing row to Quick Comparison table - Test-PolicyCompliance.ps1: filter block-only policies from break-glass exclusion check to prevent false positives - All 71 controls + template: add
tags to metadata header blocks to prevent single-paragraph rendering (CSS already expects br+strong) Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
